failedAssertions.reason: VS Code runtime not available in headless Linux VPS environment. Extension.js correctly implements diagnostic collection by spawning linter with --json flag and creating Diagnostic objects. Cannot verify actual VS Code Problems panel behavior without VS Code.

VS Code runtime not available in headless Linux VPS environment. Linter passes all sample files (exit code 0), extension.js correctly handles empty diagnostics case. Cannot verify actual VS Code Problems panel without VS Code.

notes: Round 5 of m3-vscode user-testing validation. VS Code runtime remains unavailable on this headless Linux VPS (confirmed: no 'code' binary). All 4 testable assertions (VAL-VSCODE-001, 002, 003, 006) were validated in prior rounds via code inspection — package.json manifest fields correct, extension.js correctly invokes linter with --json flag, TextMate grammar pattern accepts correct ID formats. The 2 blocked assertions (VAL-VSCODE-004, VAL-VSCODE-005) require actual VS Code runtime to verify Problems panel behavior — this is an infrastructure limitation, not an implementation defect. The vscode-extension-linter-diagnostics feature correctly implements the required behavior (confirmed via code inspection). No further testing possible without VS Code runtime — assertions deferred to m5-vscode-runtime milestone. No new features completed since prior round — no new assertions to test.
